metadata.title: /clear vs. /compact: Context Window Hygiene
metadata.description_blurb: Students keep typing into a filled context window wondering why quality drops. /clear wipes the conversation — safe because durable rules live in CLAUDE.md, not the chat. /compact summarizes when the gist still matters.

beats.narration_text: Three hours into the build. The context window is full of the failed first attempt, the corrected version, a thousand lines of test output, and two approaches they talked themselves out of. Quality is dropping. Claude is referencing a constraint they dropped two rows ago. The student thinks the model is getting worse. The model is fine. The context is not. This is Cześć, Liam — in for Bear.
beats.new_visual_element: ClaudeComposerAsk — cold open, continuing build with stale filled context

props.command: continue implementing the filter function from where we left off

beats.narration_text: The context window is the running transcript of every prompt, output, tool call, and result Claude re-reads from the top on every turn. Everything in it does two things: it consumes the budget, and it conditions the next output. The failed first attempt is still in there. The abandoned approach is still in there. They are not just eating space — they are biasing the model. Quality degrades because the context contains the problem, not because the model changed.
beats.new_visual_element: Context window — re-read every turn / consumes budget / biases next output

beats.narration_text: slash-clear wipes the conversation. The code on disk stays. CLAUDE.md stays. Project files stay. Only the chat disappears — and that is safe because durable instructions do not live in the chat. They live in CLAUDE.md, which Claude reloads at the start of the next turn. The stack, the conventions, the negative constraints, the things-never-to-improvise — all reread from the file. The transient conversation is discarded. It does not need to be preserved.
beats.new_visual_element: /clear mechanics — chat gone / code on disk stays / CLAUDE.md reloaded next turn

beats.narration_text: slash-compact summarizes the conversation instead of erasing it. Use compact when the context is long but still load-bearing — the history contains why the next output will be correct. Use clear when the context is finished and in the way — between two unrelated tasks, after repeated corrections on the same issue, after you abandoned an approach and started fresh. The test: is the conversation still doing work? Yes: compact. No: clear.
beats.new_visual_element: /compact vs /clear decision — still load-bearing → compact / finished and in the way → clear

beats.narration_text: slash-clear feels expensive. The conversation feels like progress; clearing it feels like losing progress. The progress is not in the chat — it is in the code on disk and the instructions in CLAUDE.md. If Claude has corrected the same issue more than twice, the context is polluted. Clear and rewrite the spec with the misunderstanding written out. The cleared session starts from a state where the only things Claude sees are the things you chose to put in CLAUDE.md.
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — /clear rule: twice-corrected = polluted / clear + rewrite spec

beats.narration_text: Next session, clear before you start a new task. Paste this into the fresh context.

props.command: [After /clear] Read CLAUDE.md. Then read the current state of these files: [list files relevant to next task]. Tell me what you see in these files and what you would expect the next task to involve — before I tell you what it is. This calibrates us before any change.

beats.narration_text: If this stops stale context from biasing your next row, subscribe. Liam, in for Bear.
